commands.purpose: Enumerate Users. Filter by tenant / application / status / role. Prints a table with id, email, status, tenant, default role. Ops tooling only — not run in tenant hot paths.

Print the resolution trace for one User: identity + profile + tenant memberships + effective permissions per tenant + last_login_at. --include-profile expands the PII cluster (requires the operator's account to hold profile.view.pii).

Create a User synchronously. Resolves the Identity by email (creates it if missing), invokes UserProvisioner, and prints the resulting ULID + login URL. Used for support-agent-triggered provisioning + seed scripts.
Set status=suspended + fire UserSuspended. --reactivate-at accepts an ISO 8601 timestamp; the scheduler auto-reactivates at the boundary.
Restore a soft-deleted User row within the erasure hold window. Refuses when the window has expired (the row has been purged).

GDPR Art. 17 erasure. Soft-deletes the User + schedules PurgeErasedUsersJob for `now() + user.privacy.erasure_hold_days`. --force skips the hold and hard-deletes immediately (dangerous; ops sign-off required).

commands.signature: user:switch-default-tenant {user : ULID or email} {tenant : ULID or slug}
commands.purpose: Change the User's default TenantMember. The observer enforces exactly-one-default across the User's memberships. Fires UserSwitchedActiveTenant.

CSV bulk-invite entry point. Each row: email,first_name,last_name,role_slug. Creates access::Invitation rows + dispatches ProvisionUserJob per accepted invitation. Gated by the `bulk_user_provision` entitlement + quota. --dry-run parses + validates without persisting.
Admin-side profile update. Fires ProfileUpdated with the touched fields. Refuses PII writes unless the operator carries profile.update.pii.

commands.signature: tenant-member:add {user : ULID or email} {tenant : ULID or slug} {--role=} {--staff-only}
commands.purpose: Grant the User a TenantMember row in the target Tenant. Refused when the Tenant's application_id differs from the User's application_id.

commands.signature: tenant-member:remove {user : ULID or email} {tenant : ULID or slug} {--reason=}
commands.purpose: Soft-delete the TenantMember row + revoke tokens scoped to (user, tenant). Refused when the target is the User's default AND they have no other memberships — use `user:erase` instead.
Print every membership the User holds across the caller's visible Tenants + their roles + last_active_at.
